title: rosa montana subsp. caballicensis
intro: Rosa montana subsp. caballicensis, also known as the Pyrenean rose or Rosa caballina, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the rose family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues and respiratory problems, due to its astringent and anti-inflammatory effects. The plant is rich in antioxidants and has been shown to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and exhibiting antimicrobial properties. Rosa montana subsp. caballicensis can be prepared as teas, infusions, or tinctures, and its flowers can also be used in herbal remedies and cosmetics.
